data.title: Macy's to claw back $600K in exec bonuses tied to accounting error - Bloomberg
data.description:
• Macy’s (NYSE:M) is taking back more than $600,000 in executive bonuses that resulted from an overstated earnings metric that inflated compensation, according to Bloomberg citing a filing by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • The earnings metric, which was used to calculate executive bonuses, was overstated by ~$81M in 2023 resulting in $609,613 in overpaid bonuses, of which $257,520 has already been retrieved.
  • The error was identified during an internal accounting audit last year, which revealed $154M in delivery costs that were intentionally concealed by an employee.
  • Due to the erroneous exclusion of these costs, Macy’s (NYSE:M) was able to show increased profits due to lower delivery costs. The discovery of the cost manipulation forced Macy’s (NYSE:M) to delay its third quarter results and restate its profit outlook.

data.title: TJX Companies is seen as a retail standout amid concerns about tariffs and the economy
data.description:
Jefferies sees TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX) as well-positioned for growth in the off-price retail sector.

Analyst Corey Tarlowe noted that in an environment of surplus inventory that poses markdown risks for many retailers, TJX (NYSE:TJX) could stand out by capitalizing on the conditions due to its strong operational model and market positioning.

CEO Ernie Herrman has emphasized the availability of inventory in the coming months, which presents significant buying opportunities for TJX's experienced team of over 1,300 buyers. The strategic approach is expected to enhance margins further, with FY25 gross margin reaching a peak of 30.6%, up 60 basis points year over year. Jefferies views TJX's FY26 gross margin as conservative, given the prior outperformance.

Also working in TJX's (TJX) favor, channel checks by Jefferies revealed robust in-store product availability across categories like luxury apparel, home goods, and seasonal items, reinforcing the retailer's ability to meet diverse consumer demands while driving growth and profitability.

The long-term outlook for TJX (TJX) also looks favorable to Jefferies.

"We believe TJX should benefit from the secular migration toward the Off-Price sector, which, in our view, will likely lead to share gains from other, more traditional retailers. Additionally, we believe Home and International expansion represents unique growth opportunities for TJX," highlighted Tarlowe.

Jefferies has a Buy rating on TJX (TJX) and a price target of $150 (+20% upside potential).

data.title: U.S. ETFs capture historic $296B of inflows in Q1, defying market weakness
data.description:
It was a tough first quarter for equities in 2025, but that doesn't seem to have deterred market participants from exchange-traded funds. In fact, ETFs listed in the U.S. achieved a historic milestone in Q1 by attracting a record $296B in inflows.

The amount marks a record high, surpassing the previous best of $248B set in Q1 2021, according to the latest US-listed ETF Flash Flows report from State Street Global Advisors.

ETFs pulled in record flows even as investors watched the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) drop into correction territory on concerns related to tariffs, inflation, growth expectations and ongoing geopolitical issues. 

The month of March alone saw $96B in ETF inflows, marking the ninth-largest monthly haul ever recorded. Notably, low-cost ETFs garnered $148B, while active funds accounted for $120B of the total. Continuing at this current pace, ETFs are on track to reach $1.3T in total inflows by the end of the year. 

In terms of asset classes, equity ETFs captured $67B in March, though U.S. sector ETFs saw $3B in outflows as investors reduced their exposure and looked to international markets. European ETFs stood out, accounting for 100% of international flows, bringing in $6.2B, the second-highest monthly inflow on record. 

Bond ETFs also saw observed significant activity, drawing in $21B in March. In the commodities space, ETFs tracking gold garnered $12B in Q1, pushing inflation-sensitive assets to their highest inflows since 2020. 

Highlighted below are Wall Street’s 20 largest exchange-traded funds from an asset under management perspective.

data.title: American Airlines Group set to snap six days of losses as shares trade in green
data.description:
American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL) shares set to snap six straight sessions of losses, as the stock rose over 2.6% during afternoon trade on Wednesday.

The company lost over 10.5% in the preceding six sessions.

AAL is down nearly 26% over the past one month. The stock closed 2.37% lower on Tuesday at $10.30.

The stock has lost more than 40% so far this year, compared to nearly 4% decline in the broader S&P 500 Index.

Jefferies turned cautious on the airline sector on Tuesday. The firm warned that consumer sentiment continues to disappoint and pointed out that tariffs, which take effect this week, could impact continue to impact investor confidence.

The brokerage firm downgraded American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL) to Hold from Buy.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, AAL has a Hold rating with a score of 3.22 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of valuation, while it got d in the momentum factor.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and rated the stock as a Hold.

Seeking Alpha analyst Blake Winiecki noted that most major airlines have cut forecasts with concern of economic uncertainty. 

“AAL's earnings and revenue have been sporadic, with operating income and EBITDA down in 2024, and guidance for 2025 has been slashed,” cautioned Winiecki, adding that, “economic headwinds, high debt, and lower growth compared to peers make AAL less attractive despite its low valuation.”

Turning to the Wall Street community, 13 analysts gave AAL a Buy and above, and 10 analysts gave a Hold rating. No analyst gave Sell or Strong Sell rating.

data.title: AppLovin is a bidder for TikTok - CNBC
data.description:
AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP) Corp. is a bidder for TikTok, according to a CNBC report.

AppLovin  (NASDAQ:APP) is in talks with the Trump administration over a bid for TikTok, according to CNBC's David Faber, who cited people familiar with the matter. AppLovin would not have the ability to host the servers, unlike some of the other bidders. 

"At the very least, the Trump administration is fully aware of their interest in also being a potential acquirer of TikTok," Faber said on the business network.

The latest update comes after reports earlier Wednesday that Amazon (AMZN)  made a last minute bid for TikTok. 

Companies interested in buying or taking a stake in TikTok also include Oracle (ORCL), Rumble (RUM), and Perplexity AI. Former Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin is also leading an investor group with plans to acquire TikTok. Meanwhile, Kevin O'Leary and Frank McCourt are in the mix. O'Leary, known from Shark Tank, and McCourt, a billionaire real estate tycoon, have joined forces under "The People's Bid for TikTok."

Shares of AppLovin (APP) rose 2.8% on Wednesday.

data.title: Top Quant-rated Insurance stocks with forward growth of more than 4%
data.description:
Insurance stocks (NYSEARCA:KIE), (IAK) have held up nicely during the recent equity drawdowns.

The sector saw the biggest gains last week, while sectors like fintech came under pressure, as well as the financial select sector (XLF).

The SPDR S&P Insurance ETF (NYSEARCA:KIE) s up 8% year-to-date, while the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLF) is up less than 4%.

“The insurance sector stands out as trading at a substantial valuation discount to [defensive sectors, such as utilities (XLU) and consumer staples (XLP)] sectors and the broader market despite having similar growth prospects,” according to investment publication company Blue Chip Portfolios.

“The insurance business has a number of positive attributes, including high barriers to entry, which have allowed large players to have very long staying power.”

The following are Seeking Alpha buy-rated insurance stocks (KIE) with a positive P/E GAAP trailing twelve months and a forward growth revenue of at least 4%.

Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system awards grades to stocks based on key quantitative measures, like valuation, growth and profitability.

data.title: HSBC: Economy faces headwinds with volatility, inflation, and projected Q1 earnings
data.description:
HSBC’s global research team noted that the US economy's outlook worsened in March, leading to significant market volatility. The S&P 500 (SP500) saw its largest monthly decline in over two years, driven by mounting concerns over President Trump’s aggressive tariff agenda and a slowing economic recovery.

The economic slowdown was further exacerbated by a rise in inflation, with the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index jumping to 2.54% year-over-year in February. The core PCE inflation, excluding food and energy, surged even higher to 2.8% year-over-year, up from the previous month. HSBC economists are predicting that core PCE inflation will remain above 2.5% for the remainder of 2025, signaling persistent pressure on consumer prices. 

Amid these inflationary concerns, the investment institution stated that Q1 earnings are about to kick off on Wall Street, with forecasts anticipating that earnings will slow noticeably from last quarter.

Q1 earnings projections are mixed, with a sharp 7.8% decline in earnings per share from the previous quarter, but a 7.2% increase compared to Q1 2024. Additionally, sales growth estimates for the first quarter show a 4.3% decrease from Q4 2024, although they are projected to rise 4.0% year-over-year. 

These economic strains, coupled with trade uncertainties, are casting a shadow over investor sentiment, prompting fears of a potential slowdown in US economic growth. 

In order to track the pulse on the broader market see below a grouping of market tracking and fixed income focused exchange traded funds. 

Market tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ). 

Fixed Income Funds: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (NASDAQ:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), (NYSEARCA:BIL), (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

data.title: Maersk’s biggest shareholder makes $1.3B offer for tugboat operator Svitzer
data.description:
A.P. Moeller-Maersk's (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) main shareholder said Wednesday it made an all-cash offer to acquire for Danish towage and marine-services provider Svitzer Group (OTCPK:SVZRF) that values the company at ~9B kroner ($1.3B).

A.P. Moller Holding, the Maersk family's investment arm, currently owns 47% of Svitzer (OTCPK:SVZRF) and is now offering to buy the remaining shares it does not already own.

Svitzer has a fleet of tug boats that help maneuver ships in and out of ports and terminals, and offers services such as line handling and personnel transfer, among others; it demerged from shipping giant A.P. Moeller Maersk last year and listed on Nasdaq Copenhagen.

data.title: Trump tells insiders Elon Musk will step back soon from lead government role: Politico
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has told people in his inner circle that Elon Musk will step back from his role as a governing partner in the coming weeks, Politico reported Wednesday. 

Trump and Musk in recent days had decided Musk will soon to return to his businesses and take on a supporting role in government, the report said, citing three unnamed Trump insiders. Trump remains pleased with Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency initiative, the report said. 

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares rose 5.8% to $275.75 in afternoon trade. They had fallen earlier with Tesla’s Q1 deliveries plunging. The EV maker posted 336,681 deliveries, below the consensus estimate of 377,000. 

The White House's press secretary in a Wednesday post on X (formerly Twitter) called Politico's report "garbage," and said Musk will continue DOGE work until it is "complete". 

Broadly, stocks moved higher (SP500)(COMP:IND)(DJI) in afternoon trade, ahead of the Trump administration’s announcement about potential reciprocal tariffs on trading partners worldwide.

Shares of Trump's social media company, Trump Media & Technology (NASDAQ:DJT), were down 4.6%. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Broadcom in spotlight as Daiwa upgrades to Buy on AI strength
data.description:
Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) was in the spotlight on Wednesday as investment firm Daiwa Securities upgraded the semiconductor and software firm to Buy, citing its strong position in artificial intelligence. 

Shares rose 2.5% in afternoon trading.

"We see four strong drivers for Broadcom here in 2025, and going forward into the future," analyst Louis Miscioscia wrote in a note to clients. "With the shares down 33% from recent December 2024 high, and at a valuation of 22x FY26 EPS, we find the shares attractive at these levels." Miscioscia raised his rating on Broadcom to Buy from Outperform, but lowered his price target to $225 from $275.

The four drivers that Broadcom has going for it include its application specific integrated circuit / XPU business, as Miscioscia pointed out it has gone from three core customers to seven. "The reason is that ASIC processors can be lower cost as they are designed specifically for training or inference and pull less power," Miscioscia added. "It is these benefits that Google, Meta, ByteDance, OpenAI/others are signing up."

Its networking business also stands to benefit from AI, as all of the AI factories will need to be connected, and Broadcom's status in Ethernet is strong, Miscioscia explained. He also pointed to the VMware acquisition, as it should continue to "provide growth, margins and cash flow," and the coming rebound in the broader semiconductor market.

Potential negatives include tariffs, the upcoming AI Diffusion Rule and the possibility that the AI market takes a downturn.

"Even with all this, as we wrote, we believe Broadcom shares sell off is overdone, and the company is well positioned for the future," Miscioscia wrote.
